_description: Template for your own golden self-test cases. Copy to golden-cases.json and add your validated closed PRs. Commander runs workers against these in dryrun mode and compares output. A regression means a commander prompt change made worker output meaningfully worse.
_how_to_add: After a manual Stage-0 validation of a closed PR in one of your repos, capture: repo, pr_num, parent_commit (pre-fix), key assertions (what the worker MUST produce), and minimum quality signals (tests added? no source regressions? build passes?).

cases.ticket_body_summary: Short description of what the ticket asked for, as you'd write it if re-opening the issue today

cases._comment: STUB for the retro command (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-retro-workflow.md). Populate `fixture_logs_path`, `fixture_citations_path`, and `expected_sections` with real values once you have a week of real logs. Until then, this stub documents the shape so the next operator knows what a retro golden case looks like. Runs against the commander itself (no worker subagent) — retro is an inline commander procedure, not a spawned worker.
cases.ticket_body_summary: Run `retro` against a fixed snapshot of logs/ + state/memory-citations.json + memory/escalation-faq.md and verify the output matches the template structure in CLAUDE.md (Weekly retro section).
